# Dashboard — customisable widget grid

A draggable, resizable dashboard built on `react-grid-layout` (v2) with PRIZM
components. Users toggle edit mode to rearrange, resize, add, and remove
widgets; the arrangement persists to `localStorage`. Widgets are defined as a
data **catalogue** of *kinds*; the dashboard manages which instances are
**placed** and where.

## Files

| File | Responsibility |
|------|----------------|
| `dashboard.tsx` | `<Dashboard>` — grid, edit-mode toolbar, add/remove/reset, layout state + persistence. The only stateful piece. |
| `widget.tsx` | The compound `Widget` component (`Widget`, `Widget.Header`, `Widget.Body`) + `WidgetProvider`. Chrome reads per-widget state from context — nothing is prop-drilled. |
| `types.ts` | `WidgetKind`, `WidgetCatalogue`, `DashboardWidget`. |
| `widgets/` | One file per widget kind, each exporting a `WidgetKind`. `widgets/index.ts` assembles `WIDGET_CATALOGUE` and `DEFAULT_DASHBOARD`. |
| `widgets/example.*.tsx` | The shipped demo widgets and their helpers (e.g. `example.tasks.tsx`, `example.kpi.tsx`). See the prefix convention below. |

### The `example.` prefix

Files prefixed `example.` are **throwaway demo content** — they exist to show the
system working and can all be deleted in one sweep. Real widgets you add should
**not** carry the prefix, so the two are never confused. If you remove the demos,
delete the matching imports from `widgets/index.ts` (and any `DEFAULT_DASHBOARD`
entries) too.

## Two concepts: catalogue vs. placement

- **Catalogue** (`WIDGET_CATALOGUE`): the *kinds* of widget that exist — their
  constraints and content. Drives the "Add widget" menu. Static config.
- **Placement** (`DashboardWidget[]`): which instances are currently on the
  grid and their `x/y/w/h`. Dynamic; lives in component state and `localStorage`.

A kind can have multiple placed instances (e.g. several `notes`) unless it is a
`singleton`. The `type` on a placed widget references a catalogue key.

## `<Dashboard>` API

```tsx
import { Dashboard } from "~/components/dashboard/dashboard";
import { WIDGET_CATALOGUE, DEFAULT_DASHBOARD } from "~/components/dashboard/widgets";

<Dashboard
  catalogue={WIDGET_CATALOGUE}        // required: the kinds available
  defaultWidgets={DEFAULT_DASHBOARD}  // required: initial placement (+ "Reset" target)
  storageKey="prizm-dashboard-layout" // optional: persist arrangement to localStorage
/>
```

| Prop | Type | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| `catalogue` | `WidgetCatalogue` | `Record<type, WidgetKind>`. The object key is the widget `type`. |
| `defaultWidgets` | `DashboardWidget[]` | Initial placed instances; also restored by the toolbar's "Reset". |
| `storageKey` | `string?` | If set, the arrangement is saved/restored on this device. Saved entries whose `type` is no longer in the catalogue are dropped on load. |

Grid constants live at the top of `dashboard.tsx`: `COLS = 12`, `ROW_HEIGHT = 80`,
`MARGIN = [16, 16]`. Change them there if you need a denser/looser grid.

## `WidgetKind` (types.ts)

```ts
interface WidgetKind {
  title: string;                       // shown in header AND the Add menu (single source)
  icon: ComponentType<{ className?: string }>; // a lucide-react icon
  defaultSize: { w: number; h: number };       // grid units used when newly added
  minW?: number; minH?: number;                // enforced on resize
  maxW?: number; maxH?: number;
  locked?: boolean;    // pinned: can't move/resize/remove; others flow around it
  singleton?: boolean; // only one instance allowed (greyed out in Add menu once placed)
  content: ReactNode;  // the tile, authored with the <Widget> compound (below)
}
```

Sizes are in **grid units**, not pixels (one column wide × `ROW_HEIGHT` tall per
unit). With `COLS = 12`, `w: 6` is half-width.

## The `Widget` compound component (widget.tsx)

Author a widget's `content` with these. They pull `editing` / `locked` /
`title` / `icon` / `onRemove` from context (set by the dashboard per instance),
so you never pass those yourself.

```tsx
<Widget>            {/* Card frame; adds drag cursor in edit mode */}
  <Widget.Header /> {/* drag grip + icon + title + pinned badge / remove button */}
  <Widget.Body>     {/* scrollable content area */}
    …your content…
  </Widget.Body>
</Widget>
```

- `Widget.Header` renders icon/title from context — leave it empty. Pass
  `children` to add extra controls before the remove button.
- `Widget.Header` and `Widget.Body` both accept an optional `className`.
- Keep `content` a **module-level constant** (as the existing widgets do). It's a
  stable element, so React skips re-rendering the body on drag/resize; only the
  lightweight header re-renders.

## How to add a new widget

1. Create `widgets/<name>.tsx` exporting a `WidgetKind`:

   ```tsx
   import { Gauge } from "lucide-react";
   import type { WidgetKind } from "../types";
   import { Widget } from "../widget";

   export const latency: WidgetKind = {
     title: "Latency",
     icon: Gauge,
     defaultSize: { w: 4, h: 2 },
     minW: 2,
     minH: 2,
     singleton: true,
     content: (
       <Widget>
         <Widget.Header />
         <Widget.Body>
           <p className="text-3xl font-semibold text-fg">42 ms</p>
         </Widget.Body>
       </Widget>
     ),
   };
   ```

2. Register it in `widgets/index.ts`:

   ```ts
   import { latency } from "./latency";

   export const WIDGET_CATALOGUE: WidgetCatalogue = {
     …,
     latency, // object key === the widget `type`
   };
   ```

3. (Optional) Add it to `DEFAULT_DASHBOARD` so it appears on first load:

   ```ts
   { id: "latency", type: "latency", x: 0, y: 8, w: 4, h: 2 },
   ```

   If you don't, it's still available via the "Add widget" menu.

Nothing in `dashboard.tsx`, `widget.tsx`, or the route needs to change.

### Sharing a presentational piece

If two widgets use the same visual (the demos share `Kpi` from
`example.kpi.tsx`), put it in its own file under `widgets/` and import it from
each widget. Keep widget-specific pieces inline in the widget file.

## Conventions & gotchas

- **PRIZM tokens only** — `bg-surface`, `text-fg-muted`, `bg-accent`, etc. No raw
  colours. British English in copy.
- **Constraints reflect the catalogue, not storage.** `minW/locked/...` are
  re-applied from the kind on every render, so editing a kind's constraints takes
  effect even for already-saved layouts.
- **`type` keys are persistence keys.** Renaming a catalogue key will drop that
  widget from any saved layout (handled gracefully — the tile just disappears).
  Prefer adding a new kind over renaming if users may have saved arrangements.
- **`process` shim.** `dashboard.tsx` defines `globalThis.process` on the client
  because `react-draggable` reads `process.env.DRAGGABLE_DEBUG` in its drag/resize
  handlers; without it the browser throws "process is not defined" and drag/resize
  silently break. Leave it in place.
- **SSR.** The grid mounts client-side only (`useContainerWidth({ measureBeforeMount: true })`)
  to avoid a hydration mismatch from re-measuring the container width.

## Drag/resize behaviour

- Whole card is the drag surface in edit mode; buttons, inputs, textareas, links,
  selects, and the resize handle are excluded (see `DRAG_CANCEL` in `dashboard.tsx`).
- Resize is from the bottom-right corner only (`resizeConfig.handles: ["se"]`).
- Locked widgets are `static`: immovable, no resize handle, no remove button.
